title: Tenant boundary precheck
implementation_summary: Added the control-plane Claude review Tenant boundary precheck slice for RP00.P02.M04.S03.

The S03 policy, result-field registry, prechecker module, service function, validator, synthetic fixture, contract definition, README, and RP00 validator bind a frozen metadata-only tenant-boundary result after RP00.P02.M04.S02.

The precheck consumes the S02 normalized claude_review request, requires the RP00.P02.M04.S01 service entrypoint contract to remain production_ready, and carries RP00.P02.M03.S20 Hermes Validation denied-path evidence refs forward.

The same-tenant actor/resource/Matter path emits allow_metadata_only with no blocked claim refs, while actor, resource, missing Matter tenant, or Matter tenant mismatches emit canonical blocked synthetic claim refs and deny.
The precheck enforces claude-opus-4-8, effort max, read-only review metadata, 10 review contexts, 5 required evidence refs, and 24 acceptance gates inherited through S02.

Unsafe claims fail closed, including tenant-boundary bypass, cross-tenant allowance, runtime Claude invocation, review queue writes, review assignment writes, review notification writes, service execution, runtime route creation, audit writes, product-state writes, human approval bypass, Hermes/Claude bypass, and source microphase completion.

The dedicated service tests prove policy binding, same-tenant allow behavior, firm-level null Matter behavior, actor/resource/Matter mismatch denies, unsafe-claim and wrong-source failures, prechecker no-write boundaries, and synthetic fixture parity.

The boundary is metadata-only: it does not execute service logic, create runtime routes, invoke Claude at runtime, write review queues, write review assignments, send review notifications, append audit ledgers, write audit events, mutate registries, replace human approval, write storage, write databases, or persist product state.
RP00.P02.M04 remains open after S03 and hands off to RP00.P02.M04.S04 Matter trace precheck; RP00.P02 and RP00 remain open.
